title: [lichen_l2.c:980-991 vs lora_l2.c:583-584] callback re-registration assumes stop() behavior
description: Cross-file analysis: In lichen_l2_enable() at line 980-991, the code re-registers the RX callback before calling start(), with rollback on failure. This design assumes that stop() in lora_l2.c clears the callback (line 583-584). The dependency is implicit - if stop() ever changes to NOT clear the callback, the re-registration would overwrite an existing valid callback. The comment at line 978-980 documents this, but the coupling is fragile. Consider: (1) having lichen_l2_enable() check if callback is already set before registration, or (2) making the callback clearing an explicit part of stop()'s documented contract in lora_l2.h. Currently the only documentation is in the source comment, not the header.
close_reason: Fixed: Added CONTRACT comment in lora_l2.c stop() documenting that stop() ALWAYS clears RX callback, and updated lora_l2.h lichen_lora_l2_stop() documentation to specify callers must re-register callback after restart.
